configuration.selectList.assign.descript: The new entry may be assigned to tests or it may be saved without assignment. Select one or more tests for assignment and then adjust the display order. If the new entry should be the normal range value for the selected test check the box labeled `Normal`. If it is desired that the technician should enter text with the result selection to further qualify the results then check the box labeled `Qualifiable`, For instance; if the options are a list of organisms and the last option is `Other` then the user could enter an organism not on the list.

description.active: If the test is active it can be ordered on the order form or as part of a test algorithm. If it is not active it cannot be ordered or be part of a test algorithm
description.ageRange: If the age range (in months) matters for the given values it will be specified here.
description.name: The name of the test as it will appear within openELIS. Both English and French are shown
description.normalRange: Any numeric result within this range is what is expected in a healthy person.
description.note: n/a means not available. The value is not specified.
description.orderable: If a test is active and orderable then it can be ordered on an order form. If it is active but not orderable then it will only be done if it is reflexed from another test
description.panel: If this test is part of a test panel then the panel will be named here.
description.referenceValue: The value of a selection for a healthy person. Only given for select list results.
description.reportName: The name of the test as it will appear in reports. Both English and French are shown
description.resultLimits: The limits of normal and valid results for numeric tests. The values can depend on both the age and sex of the patient.
description.resultType: The kind of result for this test:
description.resultType.alphanumericDesc: Accepts either numeric or text in a text box. It will not be evaluated for being normal or valid.
description.resultType.cascadingMultiSel: Similar to multi-select but the user will be able to select multiple groups from the dropdown list.
description.resultType.kind: The kind of result for this test:
description.resultType.multiSelectListDe: The user will be able to select one or more values from a dropdown list. No reference value will be specified.
description.resultType.numericDesc: Accepts only numeric results in a text box. Results can be evaluated as to being in a normal or a valid range.
description.resultType.selectListDesc: User will be able to select from a dropdown list. The normal value will be specified as the reference value.
description.resultType.textAreaDesc: Accepts up to 200 characters in a text area. It will not be evaluated for being normal or valid.
description.sampleType: The type of sample on which the test can be done. If the intake technician is able to select the type of sample after they have ordered the test it will be marked as user to indicate that the user will select the type
description.selectValues: Only specified for select, multi-select or cascading multi-select results. These are the available selections shown to the user. If the selection is marked as 'qualifiable' then when the user selects that value they will be able to enter additional information in a text box.
description.sex: If the sex of the patient matters for the given values it will be specified here.
description.significantDigits: The number of significant digits for numeric results. Entered results will be rounded or padded to the correct number of digits. The normal range will also be displayed with the correct number of significant digits.
description.testUnit: Which section of the lab performs the test. This is also known as a test section.
description.uom: Unit of measure for the test. This usually only applies to numeric or alphanumeric result types.
description.validRange: Any numeric result not in this range is an indication that the test may not have been done correctly.

instructions.test.activation: Active Tests: a checked box indicates an already active test. To activate a test, check the box; to deactivate, uncheck the box. instructions.test.activation.sort: Display Order: Lists of sample types which have newly activated tests. Sort the tests to indicate how they will be displayed in the order entry screen. Sorting can be done by dragging items to the desired position.
instructions.test.order: Test with checks are orderable, those without checks are not orderable.  Change checks to change status.  For a test to be able to be ordered on intake it must be both active and orderable.  Tests which are active but not orderable may be requested as part of a test reflex.

reports.nonConformity.notification.repor: If a service is selected then any non-conformity notifications which have not yet been printed will be printed. If there are no services listed then all notifications have already been printed. Entering a laboratory number will print a notification even if it has already been printed. A service does not have to be chosen.
requester.label: Requester
result.acceptasis.warning:
Checking this box will indicate that you accept the results unconditionally.
Expected uses:
1. The test has been redone and the result is the same.
2. There is no result for the test but you do not want to cancel it.
3. The result was changed and the technician wants to give the biologist the option to add a note during the validation step explaining the reason of the change.
In  either case, leave a note explaining why you are taking this action.

siteInfo.description.default.barcode: Indicate the default number of bar code labels which should be printed with every order and specimen.
siteInfo.description.dimensions: Indicate the dimensions that bar code labels should conform to when printing.

stillThere.message: User session is about to time out. Click anywhere to stay logged in.
stillThere.title: Still There?
systemuserrole.instruction.1: There are two types of Roles. Global Roles which are set for the entire application and Lab Unit Roles which can be set for all Lab Units or specific Lab Units.
systemuserrole.instruction.2: The available Global Roles are: GLobal Administrator (Acces to all Admin Functions) ,Audit Trail (Access to all Audit Trail), User Account Administrator (has acces to all the user account administration features ,but not any other admin functions).
systemuserrole.instruction.3: The Lab Unit Roles are: Reception (Access to the Order, Patient, and Non-Conformity Tabs) ,Results (Access to the Work Plan, Non-Conformity, and Results Tabs) ,Validation (Access to the Validation and Non-Conformity Tabs), Reports (Acces to Reports Tab except CSV export only Availbale for Global Administrators).
systemuserrole.instruction.4: To assign Lab Unit Roles ,select all lab units or the specific lab unit from the drop down and select any roles you would like to set.
systemuserrole.instruction.5: You can also use the Copy Permisions Feature to copy the permisions of an existing user.Ensure that you have filled all the information above and enter the user name whose permissions you want to copy into the box and click apply.
test.add.copy.name: Copy from Test Name
test.description.active: If the test is active it can be ordered on the order form or as part of a test algorithm.If it is not active it can not be ordered or be part of a test algorithm
test.description.name: The name of the test as it will appear within openELIS. Both English and French are required
test.description.orderable: If a test is active and orderable then it can be ordered on an order form. If it is active but not orderable then it will only be done if it is reflexed from another test
test.description.panel: If this test is part of a test panel then the panel can be added here.It is possible, but not usual, for a test to be in more then one panel
test.description.reportName: The name of the test as it will appear in reports. Both English and French are required.If the Name and reporting name are the same click on the button to copy the name
test.description.testSection: The test section in which the test will be done
test.description.uom: Unit of measure for the test. This usually only applies to numeric or alphanumeric result types
test.name: Test Name
test.section.label: Test Section
testUnit.existing.inactive: Inactive Test Units.  Assign tests to activate.
testcalculation.label.name: Calculation Name
testnotification.instructionis.variables: [testName] : Name of the test this is for
testnotification.instructions.body: When a notification is created, it will first check if a message template is defined for the recipient/message type.
testnotification.instructions.body.0: If none exists, it will use the test default message.
testnotification.instructions.body.1: If that does not exist, it will use the system default message.
testnotification.instructions.body.2: Variables will be replaced by their corresponding values in the message body and the subject.
testnotification.instructions.body.3: Variables should always include the square brackets and should have no whitespace.
testnotification.patiententry.info: If OpenELIS is not able to connect to the recipient of the transmitted results it will queue them up. A large and growing queue is a sign that troubling shootingshould be done including contacting the receiving party.

notebook.immunology.compilation.qualityF: Flag {count} samples with quality issues. Document the reason and determine if repeat testing is required.
notebook.immunology.compilation.exportMo: Select the data to include in the export file.
notebook.immunology.compilation.includeR: Raw Data (ELISA OD values, FCS files)
notebook.immunology.compilation.redcapMo: Upload results to a project-specific REDCap database.
notebook.page.immunology.archiving.title: Project Archiving & Conclusion
notebook.page.immunology.archiving.descr: Complete project conclusion: verify inventory, transfer samples to biorepository with complete documentation, ensure traceability, and archive all associated data.
notebook.immunology.archiving.step1.desc: Verify complete inventory of all parent samples and their child samples (aliquots, derived specimens). Ensure all samples are accounted for with current status and storage location.
notebook.immunology.archiving.step2.desc: Prepare samples for long-term storage in biorepository. Generate transfer documentation including complete sample history for each specimen.
notebook.immunology.archiving.noSamplesS: Please select samples to transfer to biorepository.
notebook.immunology.archiving.transferSu: Successfully transferred {count} samples to biorepository. Transfer document: {docId}
notebook.immunology.archiving.transferEr: Failed to transfer samples to biorepository.
notebook.immunology.archiving.step3.desc: Verify permanent storage is logged in LMIS with complete traceability links: Parent → Child relationships, all processing events, all analysis events, and storage history.
notebook.immunology.archiving.traceabili: Traceability verified for all samples. All parent-child relationships, processing events, and storage history confirmed.
Failed to verify traceability. Some samples may have incomplete history.
notebook.immunology.archiving.step4.desc: Archive all associated data including raw assay data, analysis files, QC records, and protocols used during the project.
notebook.immunology.archiving.dataArchiv: All associated data archived successfully. Archive ID: {archiveId}
notebook.immunology.archiving.transferMo: Transfer {count} samples to biorepository for long-term storage. Transfer documentation will include complete sample history.
notebook.immunology.archiving.archiveMod: Select the data types to archive for permanent storage and future reference.
notebook.immunology.archiving.archiveRaw: Raw Assay Data (ELISA plates, Flow Cytometry files)
notebook.immunology.archiving.finalModal: Verify all archiving steps are complete before finalizing. This action will mark the project as archived.
notebook.immunology.archiving.check.samp: All samples accounted for in inventory
notebook.immunology.archiving.check.trac: All traceability links confirmed in LMIS
notebook.immunology.archiving.check.arch: All data archived (assay data, QC records, protocols)
notebook.immunology.archiving.incomplete: Please complete all verification items before finalizing.
notebook.immunology.archiving.complete: Project archiving completed successfully. All samples and data have been archived with complete traceability.
notebook.immunology.archiving.warning: This action is permanent. Ensure all verification items are complete before proceeding.
